ogTitle: Kellidie Bay vs Kensington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kellidie Bay and Kensington.
heading: Kellidie Bay vs Kensington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kellidie Bay, South Australia and Kensington, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kensington has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Kellidie Bay (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kellidie Bay or Kensington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kellidie Bay has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Kensington scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kellidie Bay scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Kensington scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kellidie Bay or Kensington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kensington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
